preFix.title: Roasted beetroot soup
preFix.subtitle: Deep crimson with a sharp horseradish finish
preFix.excerpt: Beetroot roasted whole until tender, blended with stock and a little balsamic vinegar, finished with crème fraîche and grated horseradish. An intensely coloured soup with an earthy, sweet flavour and a clean, sharp finish.
preFix.sourceNotes: Roasted beetroot soup draws on Eastern European borscht traditions and modern British restaurant cooking that revived root vegetables as serious ingredients from the 1990s onwards.

content.text: Beetroot roasted whole in foil concentrates its sweetness and earthiness in a way that boiling cannot match. The

is hands-off and produces beetroot with a more intense flavour than pre-cooked vacuum-packed varieties. A small splash of balsamic vinegar in the blender cuts the sweetness and lifts the earthy quality. The horseradish crème fraîche, stirred into each bowl at serving, gives the soup its finish.

items.name: Horseradish sauce
items.prepNote: or freshly grated horseradish, to taste

content.text: Heat the oven to 200°C conventional. Wrap each beetroot individually in foil. Place on a baking tray and roast for 50 to 60 minutes until completely tender when pierced with a skewer. Leave to cool for 10 minutes.
When cool enough to handle, rub the skins off with the foil or a piece of kitchen paper. Roughly chop the beetroot. Wear gloves if you have them.
Melt {{unsalted-butter}} of butter in a saucepan over a medium-low heat. Add the {{onion}} onion and cook for 6 minutes until soft. Add the {{garlic}} of garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped roasted beetroot and pour in {{stock-vegetable}} of hot stock. Simmer for 5 minutes. Add {{balsamic-vinegar}} of balsamic vinegar.
Blend until very smooth. Taste and season with salt.
Stir {{horseradish-sauce}} of horseradish into {{creme-fraiche}} of crème fraîche. Ladle the soup into bowls and add a spoonful of horseradish crème fraîche to each.

items.fix: Increase the balsamic vinegar slightly, or add a splash of red wine vinegar. More horseradish in the crème fraîche also helps.
items.cause: Beetroot is a naturally sweet vegetable. Roasting makes it sweeter still.

items.fix: Blend and serve quickly. Beetroot colour is sensitive to prolonged heat after cooking. A splash of lemon juice brightens the colour.
items.cause: The beetroot was overcooked, or the soup was simmered too long after blending.
items.symptom: Colour is purple-brown rather than vivid crimson

items.fix: The skewer should slip in with no resistance at all. 60 minutes is often needed for medium-sized beets. Use a jug blender and blend for a full 60 seconds.
items.cause: The beetroot was not fully cooked through before blending.

items.fix: Start with a small amount and add more to taste. Creamed horseradish (from a jar) is milder and more controllable than grated fresh.
items.cause: Fresh horseradish or strong horseradish sauce can be very pungent.
items.symptom: Horseradish crème fraîche is too sharp

content.text: Beetroot soup in its blended form is a British take on the Eastern European borscht tradition. The original is a clear broth or chunky soup, often with meat; the British version is roasted, puréed, and cream-finished. It became popular in the 1990s as part of a restaurant-led revival of British root vegetables. Beetroot with horseradish is a classic Central European pairing.
